seconds_v2.desc: Top-down on a white tabletop: two small clear glass shot-glass-style containers placed at upper right; a single white cotton wick lies on the table; a hand at lower left holds a third container while reaching for a wick.
Hand begins inserting/feeding a white cotton wick down into the third clear container at lower left.
Hand continues threading the wick into the small glass; other two prepped wicked glasses sit in background.
Wick now settled inside the third glass; hand reaches across to adjust it.
Both hands at the third container, fingertips pinching the wick base — securing it at bottom of the glass.
Hand pushes the wick tab down to centre and stick it to the bottom of the small clear glass.
Hands now flat on the table beside the still-prepping glass; another wick lies between the two background glasses.
Hand returns to the small glass to position the wick centred and upright.
Hand picks the prepared container upside-down/sideways, showing the centred wick — about to set it with the other two wicked containers.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead view on a white surface; three empty clear glass cups (votive containers) sit at top while bare hands at lower left position one cup and a loose wick lies on the surface. A small channel watermark logo is in the bottom-right corner.
Three empty clear glass cups, each now with a wick laid inside, arranged across the white surface; hands momentarily out of frame.
Bare hands hold a white hot glue gun, loaded with a glue stick, and dab hot glue onto the metal tab of a wick at lower left to anchor it.
Hands continue holding the hot glue gun, applying glue to the base of a wick before pressing it into a glass cup.
Glue gun set aside; three wicked glass cups sit on the white surface, wicks now standing in them.
Bare hands press a glued wick down into the center of the bottom of a glass cup to fix it in place.
Both hands steady a glass cup and seat the wick firmly against its base.
Hands hold the glass cup and adjust the wick so it stands upright and centered.
A wick is pressed into place at the bottom of the cup; the other two wicked glass cups remain at top, watermark in the corner.

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down: two small clear glass votives sit at upper left with wicks already centred; a loose wick lies on the white table; a third empty glass sits at upper right.
A hand at lower right places another empty clear glass on the table; loose wick lies centre.
Three small clear shot-glass votives now arranged in a triangle on the white tabletop, two of them already wicked, third empty.
A hand brings in a white hot-glue gun from the lower left, nozzle aimed at the small empty glass; other hand holds a wick with tab.
Hand with the wick tab withdrawn — applies hot glue to the bottom of the wick tab using the glue gun.
Hand reaches under with the glue-tipped wick tab, about to press it into the third clear glass.
Both hands working at the bottom of the third glass; pressing the wick sustainer tab firmly to glue it in.
Fingers pinch around the tab inside the glass to hold the wick straight while glue sets.
All three glasses now wicked and centred; hand pulls back as the third wick stays upright in its glued tab.

windows.transcript: Now, measure the melted shortening in the same glass that you are going to use and mix it
windows.embed_text_v2: mix melted shortening with red crayon colorant in glass bowl, pour shortening from cup and stir, second bowl of yellow melted wax with spoon, three wicked glass cups, overhead white surface
windows.summary_v2: Bare hands measure melted shortening into a glass bowl and stir it together with red crayon colorant.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead on a white surface; three wicked clear glass cups sit at top and a glass bowl of clear melted shortening with scattered red crayon shavings sits centered. Channel watermark in bottom-right.
Same layout: bowl of clear melted shortening with red crayon bits center, three wicked glass cups above, awaiting mixing.
Static overhead; melted shortening with red colorant flecks in the glass bowl, three wicked cups at top.
A second glass bowl of yellowish melted wax/oil with a metal spoon is brought in at right by a bare hand with red nails.
Both bowls now on the surface — clear shortening with red crayon at center, yellow melted oil with spoon at right; three wicked cups above.
A bare hand tips a small glass cup over the center bowl, adding more melted shortening to the red crayon mixture.
Hand stirs and combines the melted shortening with the red crayon in the center bowl; the mixture begins turning creamy.
Hand keeps mixing the shortening-and-crayon blend in the glass bowl, the contents lightening as they emulsify.
The center bowl now holds a pale creamy mixture flecked with red; yellow melted wax with spoon stays at right, wicked cups above.

tags_v2.action: mixing melted wax with colorant and fragrance

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead on a white surface; a bare hand tips a small glass cup over the center bowl of pale creamy shortening flecked with red, a yellow melted-wax bowl with spoon at right, three wicked glass cups above. Channel watermark bottom-right.
Hands rest on the two bowls; the left bowl holds a creamy red-flecked shortening mix and the right a clear yellow melted wax with a spoon.
A hand stirs the left bowl with a spoon, blending the melted shortening, red crayon and added drops of essential oil into a thinner mixture.
Stirring continues in the left bowl; the contents look pale and translucent with small red specks, yellow wax bowl steady at right.
The spoon works the left mixture, which thickens to a creamy off-white with red flecks as it cools and combines.
Hand keeps mixing; the left bowl shows a marbled cream-and-red blend, the yellow melted wax untouched at right.
Stirring the left bowl further; the mixture is becoming a uniform pale colour while a darker spot of crayon dissolves.
Final mixing strokes in the left bowl blend the shortening, crayon and oil; yellow wax bowl with spoon remains at right.
Hands withdraw; the frame shows the bowl of clear yellow melted wax at center-right with the wicked glass cups above on the white surface.

windows.embed_text_v2: Stirring crayon colour into a glass bowl of melted red candle wax with a metal spoon; second bowl of pale yellow molten wax alongside; wicked clear glass votive containers waiting on the white tabletop.

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down: three small clear wicked glass votives sit at the top of the white tabletop; in front, two open glass bowls of melted wax — left bowl bright red wax, right bowl pale yellow wax — hand stirs the red wax with a metal spoon.
Hand continues stirring the bright red molten wax in the left glass bowl; yellow wax bowl sits to the right, wicked votives waiting at top.
Red wax shows a small white speck (likely undissolved crayon dye) on the surface as the spoon swirls it.
The spoon mixes the red wax; the crayon dye breaks down further, colour deepening uniformly.
Spoon continues circular stirring in the red wax; uniform red colour emerging.
Brief pause; hand rests on bowl edge; bright red wax now fully even, yellow wax untouched.
Hand grips spoon and resumes a few more swirls of the red wax.
Spoon withdrawn from red wax briefly; hand reaches for one of the wicked glass votives at top.
Hand brings a wicked clear glass votive down beside the red and yellow wax bowls — ready for pouring.

windows.summary_v2: Bare hands stir a bowl of bright pink melted wax and add drops of liquid colorant to intensify the shade.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead on a white surface; bare hands stir a glass bowl of vivid pink melted wax at lower left with a spoon. Three wicked glass cups and a clear melted-wax bowl sit to the right, a red wax cup with a clothespin at top-left. Channel watermark bottom-right.
Hand continues stirring the bright magenta wax in the glass bowl, blending the color evenly.
Stirring the pink melted wax with the spoon; the bowl shows a swirling vivid magenta.
Hands briefly lift away, leaving the spoon resting in the bowl of bright pink wax; clear wax bowl and wicked cups at right.
A bare hand squeezes drops of liquid dye from a small bottle into the pink wax to deepen the color.
More colorant is added from the bottle into the magenta wax bowl, intensifying the hue.
Hand sets the bottle aside and resumes stirring the now-deeper pink wax with the spoon.
Stirring blends the added dye through the bright pink melted wax in the glass bowl.
The bowl now holds a uniform vivid magenta wax with the spoon in it; wicked glass cups and clear wax bowl remain at right.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead on a white surface; a glass bowl of vivid magenta melted wax with a spoon sits at lower left, a clear melted-wax bowl and three wicked glass cups to the right, a clothespin-clipped red wax cup at top-left. Channel watermark bottom-right.
Bare hands take the pink wax bowl and stir it, the bright magenta swirling under the spoon.
Hands continue mixing the magenta melted wax to an even consistency.
Stirring the bright pink wax with the spoon; the color is now smooth and uniform.
Hand keeps blending the magenta wax in the glass bowl, ensuring the dye is fully dispersed.
Final stir of the solid-pink melted wax; clear wax bowl and wicked cups steady at right.
The magenta wax bowl sits fully mixed; a hand begins reaching toward the wicked glass cups.
A bare hand picks up an empty wicked glass cup and moves it toward the pink wax bowl to prepare for pouring.
Hand holds the empty wicked cup beside the bowl of magenta wax, ready to pour the colored wax into the container.

A hand tips the bowl of pink wax and begins pouring magenta wax into the small wicked glass cup held at lower center.
Bright pink melted wax streams from the bowl into the glass cup, filling it with the first colored layer.
The pour continues into the cup; the bowl tips further to empty the magenta wax.
Hands steady the now pink-filled glass cup at left; clear wax bowl with spoon and two empty wicked cups remain at right.
A hand holds and adjusts the wick of the cup of poured pink wax, keeping it centered.
Both hands cradle the pink-wax cup, settling the wax and wick as it begins to set.
The poured magenta wax in the glass cup is steadied; the wax surface is smooth and bright pink.
Hands hold the filled cup of pink wax with its wick upright; empty wicked cups and clear wax bowl stay to the right.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead on a white surface; a glass cup of set pale-pink wax sits at lower left while bare hands steady it. A clear bowl with melted-wax residue and spoon at right, two empty wicked cups top-right, red wax cup with clothespin top-left. Channel watermark bottom-right.
Hands set down a small glass cup filled with a red wax layer at center beside the pale-pink cup.
The cup of red wax sits at center; the pale-pink cup at left, empty wicked cups and residue bowl at right.
Static overhead: pale-pink wax cup left, red-wax cup center, empty wicked cups and spoon bowl right.
A bare hand picks up a wooden clothespin and brings it toward the rim of the red-wax cup to clip across it.
Hands position the clothespin over the red-wax cup, laying it across the top to hold the wick centered.
The clothespin is clipped across the rim of the red-wax cup, pinching the wick to keep it upright while the wax sets.
Hands adjust the clothespin-and-wick on the red-wax cup so the wick stays centered as it cures.
A hand points to the finished setup: red-wax cup with clothespin holding the wick, pale-pink wax cup at left, spoon bowl and empty cups at right.

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down: two small clear glass votives at upper left now have a poured red wax bottom layer; wicks held centred by a wooden clothespin across each rim. Lower right shows a pink wax bowl with a spoon; lower left empty area.
A glass bowl of pale yellow molten wax appears at lower centre; pink wax bowl with spoon at right; red-bottom votives setting at top.
Hand at left reaches in; pale yellow wax bowl in centre with a small white speck of dye; pink wax bowl at right.
Hand begins stirring the pale yellow wax in the central bowl with the metal spoon to dissolve the dye flake.
Spoon swirls the yellow wax — colour deepening to pale gold; pink wax sits untouched at right.
Continued stirring of the yellow wax with the spoon; red-bottomed votives still curing above.
Yellow wax now uniform pale gold; spoon held in the bowl by the hand.
Hand continues a gentle swirl in the yellow wax; very faint cream colour now showing as the wax begins to thicken/cool.
Wax in central bowl has cooled to a pale cream — surface skinning over; hand still holds spoon inside.
